simt_parameters.intervention: Cells will be exposed to continuous white LED light (400-700 nm, 100 µmol photons/m²/s) to activate photosynthetic ATP production, calibrated to match basal mitochondrial ATP production (determined by preliminary ATP measurements in control HDFs). Control groups include: 1) HDFs with photosynthetic system but no light, 2) Wild-type HDFs with light, 3) Wild-type HDFs without light (glucose-dependent baseline). All groups maintained in glucose-free, pyruvate-containing DMEM to emphasize ATP source dependency. Light exposure will be continuous for 72 hours post-synchronization.
simt_parameters.meter: Real-time luminescence will be measured every 30 minutes for 72 hours using a multi-well plate reader (e.g., BMG LABTECH CLARIOstar Plus) equipped with temperature and CO2 control. Luminescence data will be normalized to total protein content (BCA assay at endpoint) and analyzed for oscillation amplitude, phase, and period using CircWave or JTK_CYCLE software. ATP levels will be quantified at 0, 24, 48, 72h using a luminescence-based ATP assay (Promega CellTiter-Glo 2.0) to confirm ATP equivalence.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: A significant alteration (p<0.05, two-way ANOVA with post-hoc Tukey's HSD) in oscillation amplitude (≥25% change) or phase (≥3-hour shift) of any circadian reporter gene in photosynthetic ATP-supplied cells compared to glucose-dependent controls, observed over 3 consecutive circadian cycles (72h), would indicate disruption. Conversely, no significant difference would suggest successful integration. ATP equivalence must be maintained within ±10% of basal mitochondrial ATP levels.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment will directly test if continuous photosynthetic ATP delivery, even at equivalent rates, perturbs the amplitude or phase of core circadian clock genes, thereby confirming or refuting the hypothesis that a constant, non-native energy source disrupts the cell's inherent pulsatile energy management. If significant changes are observed, it suggests metabolic dissonance (IH_disruption); if not, it supports seamless integration (IH_integration).

l6_tasks.title: Multi-omics Analysis of Circadian Metabolome and Transcriptome under Photosynthetic vs. Mitochondrial ATP Supply

simt_parameters.system: Engineered human dermal fibroblasts (HDFs, ATCC PCS-201-012, passages 5-8) stably transduced with the chloroplast-mimicking system. Cells will be synchronized with Dexamethasone (100 nM, 2h) and cultured in glucose-free, pyruvate-containing DMEM. Sample size: n=6 biological replicates per condition per time point.
simt_parameters.intervention: Cells will be exposed to continuous white LED light (400-700 nm, 100 µmol photons/m²/s) for photosynthetic ATP production, calibrated to match basal mitochondrial ATP. Control groups: 1) Wild-type HDFs in glucose-containing DMEM (baseline), 2) Wild-type HDFs in glucose-free DMEM (negative control for ATP). Samples will be collected every 6 hours over 48 hours (0, 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, 36, 42, 48h) post-synchronization.
simt_parameters.meter: At each time point, cells will be rapidly quenched for metabolomics (LC-MS/MS, untargeted analysis of central carbon metabolism, amino acids, and lipids) and RNA extracted for bulk RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq, >30M reads/sample). Circadian rhythmicity of metabolites and transcripts will be analyzed using MetaboAnalyst and JTK_CYCLE, respectively. Key metrics: amplitude, phase, and period of oscillating features. ATP levels will be confirmed at each time point by luminescence assay.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: A significant difference (FDR < 0.05, |log2FC| > 1 for transcripts; p<0.05 for metabolites) in the number of oscillating genes/metabolites, or a phase shift (≥3 hours) in the peak expression/concentration of at least 25% of known circadian-regulated genes/metabolites, in photosynthetic ATP-supplied cells compared to glucose-dependent controls, observed over 2 circadian cycles (48h), would indicate metabolic rhythm disruption. ATP equivalence must be maintained within ±10% of basal mitochondrial ATP levels throughout the experiment.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This multi-omics approach provides a comprehensive view of how photosynthetic ATP influences the entire circadian metabolic and transcriptional landscape. It will reveal if the continuous ATP supply perturbs not only core clock genes but also their downstream effectors and metabolic outputs, thereby providing strong evidence for or against metabolic dissonance (IH_disruption vs IH_integration).

l6_tasks.title: Time-resolved Measurement of Circadian Enzyme Activity and Redox State under Photosynthetic ATP

simt_parameters.system: Engineered human dermal fibroblasts (HDFs, ATCC PCS-201-012, passages 5-8) stably transduced with the chloroplast-mimicking system. Cells will be synchronized with Dexamethasone (100 nM, 2h) and cultured in glucose-free, pyruvate-containing DMEM. Sample size: n=5 biological replicates per condition per time point.
simt_parameters.intervention: Cells will be exposed to continuous white LED light (400-700 nm, 100 µmol photons/m²/s) for photosynthetic ATP production, calibrated to match basal mitochondrial ATP. Control groups: 1) Wild-type HDFs in glucose-containing DMEM (baseline), 2) Wild-type HDFs in glucose-free DMEM (negative control for ATP). Samples will be collected every 4 hours over 48 hours (0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 36, 40, 44, 48h) post-synchronization.
simt_parameters.meter: Cell lysates will be prepared for enzymatic assays: Phosphofructokinase (PFK) activity (Sigma-Aldrich MAK093), Pyruvate Dehydrogenase (PDH) activity (Abcam ab110332), and Citrate Synthase (CS) activity (Sigma-Aldrich CS0720). NAD+/NADH and GSSG/GSH ratios will be quantified using colorimetric/fluorometric assays (Abcam ab65348, ab112172). All measurements will be performed according to manufacturer's protocols. Data will be analyzed for circadian oscillations using JTK_CYCLE or similar software to determine amplitude, phase, and period.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: A significant dampening (≥30% reduction in amplitude) or phase shift (≥4 hours) in the circadian oscillation of at least two of the measured enzyme activities or redox ratios in photosynthetic ATP-supplied cells, compared to glucose-dependent controls, over 2 circadian cycles (48h), would indicate disruption of metabolic rhythm. ATP equivalence must be maintained within ±10% of basal mitochondrial ATP levels.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment directly assesses the functional consequences of continuous photosynthetic ATP on key metabolic pathway activities and cellular redox state, which are known to oscillate. If these oscillations are significantly altered, it supports the hypothesis of metabolic dissonance (IH_disruption); if they remain robust, it suggests successful integration (IH_integration).

l6_tasks.title: Fluxomics Analysis of Central Carbon Metabolism under Photosynthetic vs. Mitochondrial ATP Supply

simt_parameters.system: Engineered human dermal fibroblasts (HDFs, ATCC PCS-201-012, passages 5-8) stably transduced with the chloroplast-mimicking system. Cells will be synchronized with Dexamethasone (100 nM, 2h) and cultured in glucose-free, pyruvate-containing DMEM. Sample size: n=4 biological replicates per condition per time point.
simt_parameters.intervention: Cells will be exposed to continuous white LED light (400-700 nm, 100 µmol photons/m²/s) for photosynthetic ATP production, calibrated to match basal mitochondrial ATP. Control groups: 1) Wild-type HDFs in glucose-containing DMEM (baseline), 2) Wild-type HDFs in glucose-free DMEM (negative control for ATP). At 12h and 36h post-synchronization (representing different circadian phases), cells will be pulsed for 1 hour with 13C-labeled glucose (U-13C-glucose, Cambridge Isotope Laboratories, 2.5 mM) or 13C-labeled pyruvate (U-13C-pyruvate, Cambridge Isotope Laboratories, 5 mM) to trace metabolic flux.
simt_parameters.meter: After the 1-hour pulse, cells will be rapidly quenched, metabolites extracted, and analyzed by GC-MS/MS or LC-MS/MS for isotopic enrichment in key intermediates of glycolysis, TCA cycle, and pentose phosphate pathway. Metabolic flux will be quantified using computational fluxomics tools (e.g., INCA). The amplitude and phase of flux oscillations (comparing 12h vs 36h time points) will be assessed.
simt_parameters.threshold_time: A significant alteration (p<0.05, two-way ANOVA) in the relative flux through key metabolic pathways (e.g., glycolytic flux, TCA cycle flux) or a dampening (≥20% reduction) of the phase-dependent difference in flux between the 12h and 36h time points in photosynthetic ATP-supplied cells, compared to glucose-dependent controls, would indicate disruption of metabolic rhythm. ATP equivalence must be maintained within ±10% of basal mitochondrial ATP levels during the pulse.

l6_tasks.expected_impact: This experiment provides direct evidence of how photosynthetic ATP impacts the dynamic flow of carbon through central metabolic pathways. Disruption of circadian flux patterns would strongly support the hypothesis that continuous ATP delivery creates metabolic dissonance (IH_disruption), even if overall ATP levels are maintained. Conversely, preserved flux oscillations would support seamless integration (IH_integration).
